chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA Criminal Acquittal Appeal No.S-13 of 2018 Appellant/Complainant: Sobhraj s/o Lachman Dass in person, Respondents: The State through Mr.Raja Imtiaz Ali Solangi, A.P.G Private respondent Nazir Ahmed in person Date of hearing: 31.08.2018 Date of decision: 31.08.2018 J U D G M E N T IRSHAD ALI SHAH. J- The appellant by way of instant Criminal Acquittal Appeal has impugned judgment dated 27.02.2018, of learned Civil Judge & J.M-I, Qamber, whereby he has acquitted the private respondent of the offence, for which he was charged. 2. The facts in brief necessary for disposal of instant acquittal appeal are well disclosed in the FIR, which was lodged by appellant/ complainant Sobhraj with P.S Qamber City, which reads as under; “Complaint is that I am practicing Advocate of Sessions Court Qamber and High Court Larkana. I am Trustee of “Darbar Wali Wilayat Rai Sahib”. The property owned by “Darbar Sahib” is being looked-after by me. One shop belonging to “Darbar Sahib”, was on rent with Nazir Ahmed Gopang, he failed to pay its rent for considerable time. The said shop then was got vacated through Koural Shah and Haji Arbab Gopang, who were guarantors for Nazir Ahmed Gopang. The shop was in my possession. Today, I with my grandson Advocate Bantilal son of Ramesh Kumar went to the said shop, there we reached at about 1800 hours, there we found accused Nazir Ahmed with danda and Sajjad Ahmed with pistol, both sons of Roshan Gopang, residents of Latif Colony Qamber, to have encroached upon the said shop by break-opening its lock and keeping articles therein. I asked from them as to why they have encroached upon my shop by break-opening the lock, on that they used criminal force against me.

Today, I with my grandson Advocate Bantilal son of Ramesh Kumar went to the said shop, there we reached at about 1800 hours, there we found accused Nazir Ahmed with danda and Sajjad Ahmed with pistol, both sons of Roshan Gopang, residents of Latif Colony Qamber, to have encroached upon the said shop by break-opening its lock and keeping articles therein. I asked from them as to why they have encroached upon my shop by break-opening the lock, on that they used criminal force against me. I raised cries. On my cries, Advocate Suhendar Kumar s/o Heeralal Hindu, r/o Shiwala Muhalla Qamber, came running. I was rescued. Accused then went away by threatening me of my murder by pointing their pistol and applying lock on the said shop. It is complained that accused in furtherance of their common intention have encroached upon my shop and have threatened me of murder. Action be taken”. 3. After usual investigation the private respondent was challaned by the police before the Court of law. At trial, the private respondent did not plead guilty and prosecution to the charge examined PW-01 appellant/complainant Sobhraj, he produced attested copy of order of learned 3rd Additional District Judge, Larkana, Extract From Property Register Card and FIR of the present case, PW-02 Bantilal, PW-03 Mashir Mithomal, PW-04 SIO/ASI Ahmed Khan Mangnejo, PW-05 Mashir HC Muhammad Nawaz Soomro, produced through him memo of arrest of the private respondent, PW-06 Mashir PC Azizullah Channo and then closed the side. 4. The private respondent in his statement recorded under section 342 Cr.PC denied the prosecution allegation by pleading innocence. He did not examine anyone in his defence or himself on oath in disproof of the prosecution allegation. 5. On evaluation of the evidence, so produced by the prosecution, the learned trial Magistrate acquitted the private respondent of the offence for which he was charged by way of judgment dated 27.02.2018, by way of instant criminal acquittal appeal. 6. It is contended by learned counsel for the appellant that the learned Magistrate has acquitted the private respondent of the charge without any lawful justification and improper appreciation of evidence.

On evaluation of the evidence, so produced by the prosecution, the learned trial Magistrate acquitted the private respondent of the offence for which he was charged by way of judgment dated 27.02.2018, by way of instant criminal acquittal appeal. 6. It is contended by learned counsel for the appellant that the learned Magistrate has acquitted the private respondent of the charge without any lawful justification and improper appreciation of evidence. By contending so, he sought for admission of the instant criminal acquittal appeal to its regular hearing for further action against the private respondent. 7. It is contended by private respondent in person that he has been involved in this case falsely by the appellant/complainant only to compel him to vacate the shop in his possession without any lawful justification. By contending so, he sought for dismissal of the instant criminal acquittal appeal. 8. Learned A.P.G has supported the impugned judgment. 9. I have considered the above arguments and perused the record. 10. As per narration made by the appellant/complainant in his FIR, the private respondent was his tenant on shop, which belongs to “Darbar Wali Wilayat Rai Sahib”, and it was got vacated by him from the private respondent through Koural Shah and Haji Arbab on account his failure to make regular payment of rent. No tenancy agreement is produced by the appellant/complainant, which may prove tenancy. If for the sake of arguments it is believed that the appellant/complainant got restored the shop through Koural Shah and Haji Arbab then to prove such allegation, he was under lawful obligation to have examined Koural Shah or Haji Arbab. Their non examination has made the allegation of the appellant/complainant that he got restored the shop from the private respondent through Koural Shah and Haji Arbab to be doubtful. PW Suhendar Kumar, who allegedly rescued the appellant/complainant has not been examined by the prosecution for no obvious reason. The inference which could be drawn of his non examination would be that he was not going to support the case of prosecution.

PW Suhendar Kumar, who allegedly rescued the appellant/complainant has not been examined by the prosecution for no obvious reason. The inference which could be drawn of his non examination would be that he was not going to support the case of prosecution. PW Bantilal could hardly be relied upon being grandson of appellant/ complainant. In that situation the contention of the private respondent that he has been involved in this case falsely by the appellant/complainant to compel him to vacate the shop in his possession without lawful justification could not be lost sight of. In these circumstances, the learned trial Magistrate was right to record acquittal of the private respondent of the charge by extending him benefit of doubt. 11. In case of Faheem Ahmed Farooq vs.The State (2008 SCMR-1572), it is held that; “single infirmity creating reasonable doubt regarding truth of the charge makes the whole case doubtful. 12. It is settled by now that the acquittal carry with it double presumption of innocence and interference with acquittal is narrow and limited, which could only be interfered with when the judgment of the acquittal is found to have been passed in arbitrary and cursory manner. 13. In case of State and others vs. Abdul Khaliq and others (PLD 2011 SC-554), it is held by the Hon’ble Court that; “The scope of interference in appeal against acquittal is most narrow and limited, because in an acquittal the presumption of innocence is significantly added to the cardinal rule of criminal jurisprudence, that an accused shall be presumed to be innocent until proved guilty; in other words, the presumption of innocence is doubled. The courts shall be very slow in interfering with such an acquittal judgment, unless it is shown to be perverse, passed in gross violation of law, suffering from the errors of grave misreading or non-reading of the evidence; such judgments should not be lightly interfered and heavy burden lies on the prosecution to rebut the presumption of innocence which the accused has earned and attained on account of his acquittal.

Abdul Khaliq and others (PLD 2011 SC-554), it is held by the Hon’ble Court that; “The scope of interference in appeal against acquittal is most narrow and limited, because in an acquittal the presumption of innocence is significantly added to the cardinal rule of criminal jurisprudence, that an accused shall be presumed to be innocent until proved guilty; in other words, the presumption of innocence is doubled. The courts shall be very slow in interfering with such an acquittal judgment, unless it is shown to be perverse, passed in gross violation of law, suffering from the errors of grave misreading or non-reading of the evidence; such judgments should not be lightly interfered and heavy burden lies on the prosecution to rebut the presumption of innocence which the accused has earned and attained on account of his acquittal. Interference in a judgment of acquittal is rare and the prosecution must show that there are glaring errors of law and fact committed by the Court in arriving at the decision, which would result into grave miscarriage of justice; the acquittal judgment is perfunctory or wholly artificial or a shocking conclusion has been drawn. Judgment of acquittal should not be interjected until the findings are perverse, arbitrary, foolish, artificial, speculative and ridiculous. The Court of appeal should not interfere simply for the reason that on the reappraisal of the evidence a different conclusion could possibly be arrived at, the factual conclusions should not be upset, except when palpably perverse, suffering from serious and material factual infirmities”. 14. Nothing has been brought on record which may suggest that the impugned judgment has been passed by learned trial Magistrate in arbitrary or cursory manner, which may call for interference by this Court by way of instant criminal acquittal appeal, it is dismissed accordingly. JUDGE --
